data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The strict-win regime exists only in a narrow intermediate band of base error (roughly 0.55 to 0.75 at k=5) combined with high critic recall (phi > 0.7) and moderate precision (psi > 0.3); at lower p, SC is already correct (nothing to fix); at higher p, no surviving correct minority remains to benefit from filtering; the strict-loss regime emerges when psi < 0.2 and SC was right, because false flags fragment the correct majority.", "mechanism": "At k=5 with p=0.55 (3 wrong, 2 correct typically), a critic with phi=0.7 flags ~2 of 3 wrong candidates, dropping the wrong count to ~1 while the 2 correct survive (if psi=0.3, ~0.4 correct are falsely flagged, so ~1.6 survive), and harness elects the correct answer. At p=0.75 (4 wrong, 1 correct), even perfect recall leaves 0-1 wrong candidates but only 1 correct, so filtering cannot create a correct plurality — at best a tie. At p=0.15 with low psi=0.15, the 4-5 correct candidates suffer ~0.6-0.75 false flags, and if even one wrong candidate survives unflagged, a 3-correct-vs-1-wrong majority can become 2-vs-1, still correct; but at k=7, p=0.15, psi=0.15, the 6 correct candidates suffer ~0.9 false flags, and 1 wrong candidate survives, giving 5.1-vs-1, still correct — so the loss mode requires psi low enough that false flags exceed the majority margin. The loss condition is: (1-psi) * n_correct > n_correct - n_wrong, i.e., false flags on correct candidates exceed the SC majority margin.", "scope": {"covers": ["p in (0.5, 0.8) at k=5", "phi > 0.6", "psi in (0.2, 0.5)", "k=5", "loss regime: p < 1/k, psi < majority_margin / n_correct"], "excludes": ["p > 0.8 (no surviving correct minority)", "p < 0.3 with psi > 0.3 (false flags too few to erode majority)", "k=2 (no meaningful plurality dynamics)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "At p=0.65, phi=0.75, psi=0.35, k=5, harness accuracy is NOT at least 8 percentage points above SC accuracy", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At p=0.15, psi=0.1, k=5, harness accuracy is NOT at least 3 percentage points below SC accuracy, with at least 2 right-to-wrong flips from false flags", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At p=0.85, k=5, harness accuracy exceeds SC accuracy by more than 2 percentage points despite phi=1.0", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This candidate makes a concrete quantitative band prediction with specific thresholds, making it highly falsifiable. The experiment uses task-difficulty scaling to hit p=0.65 and p=0.85, and critic-prompt aggressiveness to hit psi=0.35 vs psi=0.10. The key risk is that p and psi may not be independently controllable on this provider."}
